claim: Agentic workflows differ from traditional automations by allowing the AI system to reason and adapt rather than requiring manual step-by-step configuration of tasks
actionable: Design workflows by stating desired outcomes rather than step-by-step instructions; let the AI handle task sequencing and error recovery automatically
source_title: From Zero to Your First Agentic AI Workflow in 26 Minutes (Claude Code)

claim: The WAT framework (Workflows, Agent, Tools) provides structure: Workflows define instructions in markdown, Agents coordinate decisions, Tools execute specific actions
actionable: Organize your agentic projects using three layers: Workflows (markdown instructions), Agent (the LLM coordinator), and Tools (Python functions for actions)
source_title: From Zero to Your First Agentic AI Workflow in 26 Minutes (Claude Code)

claim: Agents automatically improve their own code when errors occur by reading error messages, consulting documentation, fixing scripts, and updating workflows
actionable: When your agent encounters errors, let it handle the debugging: read the error, fix the code, test, and update the workflow to prevent the same error recurring
source_title: From Zero to Your First Agentic AI Workflow in 26 Minutes (Claude Code)

claim: Deterministic tasks (predictable, repetitive work) suit traditional automation; non-deterministic tasks (variable, judgment-requiring) suit agentic workflows
actionable: Use agentic workflows for research, content creation, customer support, and lead generation where variability and judgment are required at each step
source_title: From Zero to Your First Agentic AI Workflow in 26 Minutes (Claude Code)

claim: Use the claude.md file as an onboarding document to teach the agent how to work with your project structure, error handling expectations, and self-improvement loop
actionable: Create a claude.md file that explains your WAT framework, folder structure, file organization, and how the agent should behave when errors occur
source_title: From Zero to Your First Agentic AI Workflow in 26 Minutes (Claude Code)

claim: Brand assets (logos, guidelines) can be dropped directly into the project; the agent extracts colors, typography, and design details to apply them to generated PDFs
actionable: Include brand asset files in your project folder; Claude will automatically extract and apply branding to outputs without manual configuration
source_title: From Zero to Your First Agentic AI Workflow in 26 Minutes (Claude Code)

claim: API key cost and caching: first run costs more (~$1.50); subsequent runs cache previous data and only cost ~$0.50 due to prompt caching on 30-day cycles
actionable: Plan budget assuming first run costs will be higher; subsequent runs of the same workflow save significantly through caching, reducing ongoing operational costs
source_title: From Zero to Your First Agentic AI Workflow in 26 Minutes (Claude Code)

claim: Saving business information to JSON files (e.g., business_profile.json) allows the agent to retain context across runs and avoids re-asking the same questions
actionable: Instruct your agent to save business context and competitor data to JSON files; the agent will automatically update and reference these files in future runs
source_title: From Zero to Your First Agentic AI Workflow in 26 Minutes (Claude Code)

claim: Iterative refinement is necessary; first outputs may have issues (e.g., logos not visible, missing charts); request fixes and re-run without re-researching due to caching
actionable: Expect to run workflows multiple times; identify issues in outputs, request fixes via natural language, and re-run—cached data means only improvements run
source_title: From Zero to Your First Agentic AI Workflow in 26 Minutes (Claude Code)
